GHOST Memory Management Requirements
Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    This document defines the top-level Memory Management capability of GHOST.

    Memory Management preserves accepted interaction history as durable memory truth, creates retrieval representations, selects query-relevant memory, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief continuity, and synthesizes runtime Memory Context for SuperPrompt composition.

    Detailed atomic behavior is allocated to files 51, 52, and 53.
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor UID: GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-FUT-ROOT GHOST Future Vision and Lifecycle Synchronization Scope
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      • GHOST-AGENT-STACK-ROOT Agent Stack Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      • GHOST-KM-ROOT Knowledge Management Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-INGEST-ROOT Document Ingestion Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
      • GHOST-EVIDENCE-ROOT Evidence Selection and Condensation Scope
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT Memory Recording and Files Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope

      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Human Workbench and Interface Scope
      • GHOST-RUN-ROOT GHOST Runtime Platform and Operations Scope
      • GHOST-QGO-ROOT GHOST Quality, Governance, and Observability Scope
      STATEMENT: This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

    Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

      The current Python package namespace may remain ragstream in implementation paths.

      Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS
      1.3. Memory Management Child Documents UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The Memory Management requirement family shall be decomposed into:
      1. 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
      2. 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
      3. 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc.

      Each child shall own atomic requirements for its assigned capability and shall not duplicate sibling ownership.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE
      1.4. Requirement Nature UID: GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: This document shall define approved behavior, interfaces, boundaries, artifacts, configuration authority, and verification expectations.

      It shall not be used as an implementation-status log or as permission to infer unimplemented behavior.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES
      1.5. Current Executable Memory Capabilities UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The current executable Memory Management scope shall include manual pair capture, memory-history files, editable metadata, SQLite indexing, ChatGPT shared-link import, memory-vector ingestion, working/episodic/semantic/Direct Recall selection, ActiveRetrievalBrief maintenance, runtime episodic compression, memory synthesis, and SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION
      1.6. No Final Answer Generation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Memory Management shall produce supporting runtime context and shall not answer the user request or own final LLM answer generation.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION
      2.1. Memory Architectural Position UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-DEPENDENCY PreProcessing Dependency
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FINAL-COMPOSITION Final Prompt Composition Boundary
      STATEMENT: Memory Management shall consume the prepared SuperPrompt after PreProcessing and shall produce a memory-specific runtime context for final prompt composition.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-STACK-BOUNDARY
      2.9. Agent Stack Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-STACK-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: ActiveBrief summarization and Memory Context synthesis shall use the neutral Agent Stack defined in 20_agent_stack.sdoc.

      This Memory requirement family shall own the concrete memory payloads, decisions, safeguards, and state write-back.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-GUI-BOUNDARY
      2.10. GUI Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-GUI-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The GUI may trigger, display, or edit memory operations, but durable-memory semantics and retrieval policy shall remain owned by server-side memory components.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY
      3.1. Memory Recording Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE Current Capture Sequence
      STATEMENT: Memory Recording shall create and maintain MemoryRecord objects, active history identity, append-only stable-body files, editable metadata files, and a SQLite metadata mirror.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-FILE-CAPABILITY
      3.2. Memory File Lifecycle Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-FILE-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Memory file operations shall list, create, load, rename, and delete memory histories using stable file_id identity and mutable filenames.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-IMPORT-CAPABILITY
      3.3. Conversation Import Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-IMPORT-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The current import capability shall convert one public ChatGPT shared-link conversation into ordinary MemoryRecord objects and persist it as an ordinary memory history.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-INGESTION-CAPABILITY
      3.4. Memory Ingestion Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-INGESTION-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Memory Ingestion shall derive record-handle, question, and answer vector entries from durable MemoryRecord content and store them in the dedicated memory vector store.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY
      3.5. Memory Retrieval Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE Current Retrieval Sequence
      STATEMENT: Memory Retrieval shall combine semantic-vector results with deterministic SQLite/.ragmem lookups into a structured MemoryContextPack.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPABILITY
      3.6. ActiveRetrievalBrief Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief maintenance shall create or update a compact query-independent conversation brief when a MemoryRecord is captured and shall preserve contributor traceability.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-COMPRESSION-CAPABILITY
      3.7. Runtime Compression Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-COMPRESSION-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Runtime compression shall reduce selected episodic Q/A evidence against the effective query without modifying durable MemoryRecord text.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-CAPABILITY
      3.8. Memory Context Synthesis Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Memory synthesis shall produce one compact query-relevant memory_context string from the current configured evidence inputs.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL
      4.1. Memory Truth Model UID: GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-STABLE-BODY-AUTHORITY Stable Body Authority
      • GHOST-MEMORY-METADATA-AUTHORITY Editable Metadata Authority
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SQLITE-MIRROR SQLite Mirror Status
      • GHOST-MEMORY-VECTOR-DERIVATIVE Vector Store Derivative Status
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PACK-RUNTIME-ONLY MemoryContextPack Runtime Status
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONTEXT-RUNTIME-ONLY Synthesized Memory Context Status
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PENDING-BUFFER-RUNTIME Pending Topic Buffer Status
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ONE-ACTIVE-HISTORY Single Active Memory History
      • GHOST-MEMORY-IDENTITY-SEPARATION File Identity and Filename Separation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PARENT-TRACE Record Parent Trace
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-TRUTH AC: Durable Truth Separation
      STATEMENT: Full accepted input/output MemoryRecord content in .ragmem shall remain the durable memory-body authority.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-WORKING-ROLE
      5.1. Working Memory Candidate Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-WORKING-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Working memory shall represent the latest configured non-excluded records from the active memory history through deterministic SQLite lookup.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-EPISODIC-ROLE
      5.2. Episodic Candidate Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-EPISODIC-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Episodic memory shall represent parent MemoryRecords selected from semantic parent scoring and the configured Gold priority lookup.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SEMANTIC-ROLE
      5.3. Semantic Chunk Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SEMANTIC-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Semantic memory chunks shall represent selected question or answer vector hits with record traceability, semantic score, and optional recency contribution.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DIRECT-ROLE
      5.4. Direct Recall Candidate Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DIRECT-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Direct Recall shall represent at most one exact-key lookup result across memory histories, subject to tag exclusion and configured enablement.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE
      5.5. ActiveRetrievalBrief Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP ActiveBrief Anti-Duplication Reference
      STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief shall provide query-independent working-conversation orientation and shall remain distinct from query-dependent Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIZED-ROLE
      5.6. Synthesized Memory Context Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIZED-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-SYNTHESIS-INPUTS Current Synthesis Input Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-RAW-PACK-DEFAULT-RENDER No Raw Pack Rendering by Default
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-OVERLAP-SCOPE Current Overlap Handling Scope
      STATEMENT: Synthesized Memory Context shall be the memory artifact rendered in the normal SuperPrompt supporting-context preview.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-CORE
      6.1. Core Recording Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-CORE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current core recording implementation shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/memory/memory_record.py;
      - ragstream/memory/memory_manager.py;
      - ragstream/memory/memory_actions.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-STORAGE
      6.2. File Storage Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-STORAGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current history-file lifecycle implementation shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/storage/memory_file_manager.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-IMPORT
      6.3. Importer Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-IMPORT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current ChatGPT shared-link import implementation shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/memory/importers/chatgpt_shared_link_importer.py;
      - ragstream/memory/importers/chatgpt_shared_link_importer_helpers.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-INGESTION
      6.4. Ingestion Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-INGESTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current memory-ingestion implementation shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_chunker.py;
      - ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_ingestion_manager.py;
      - ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_vector_store.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-RETRIEVAL
      6.5. Retrieval Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-RETRIEVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current memory-retrieval implementation shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py;
      - ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_context_pack.py;
      - ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_index_lookup.py;
      - ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_scoring.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-COMPRESSION
      6.6. Compression and ActiveBrief Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-COMPRESSION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current compression and ActiveBrief implementation shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_sentence_reducer.py;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_compressor.py;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_active_retrieval_brief.py;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_activebrief_relevance_gate.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS
      6.7. Synthesis Location UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current Memory Context synthesis shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/memory_merge_synthesizer.py and data/agents/memory_synthesizer/memory_synthesizer_001.json.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS
      6.8. ActiveBrief Agent Configuration Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current ActiveBrief LLM configurations shall be traceable to:
      - data/agents/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer_init_001.json;
      - data/agents/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer_update_001.json.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI
      6.9. Runtime and GUI Wiring Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Current startup, action, and pipeline wiring shall be traceable to:
      - ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py;
      - ragstream/app/ui_actions.py;
      - ragstream/app/ui_actions_files.py;
      - ragstream/app/pipeline_runner.py;
      - ragstream/app/controller.py.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA
      6.10. Runtime Data Locations UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The current runtime data layout shall use:
      - data/memory/files/ for .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files;
      - data/memory/memory_index.sqlite3 for the SQLite mirror;
      - data/memory/vector_db/ for the persistent memory vector database.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT
      7.1. Runtime Configuration Source UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-SECTIONS Current Memory Configuration Sections
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-VALIDATION Configuration Validation Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TUNABLES-JSON-DIRECTION Tunable Parameter JSON Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-PYTHON-DEFAULTS Current Python Defaults
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEFINED-NOT-CONSUMED Defined but Unconsumed Settings
      • GHOST-MEMORY-STARTUP-EMBEDDER Current Memory Vector Embedder
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REDUCER-EMBEDDER Current Reduction Embedder
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-VERSIONS Versioned Memory Agent Selection
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-OBSERVABILITY Effective Configuration Observability
      STATEMENT: Current memory retrieval, compression, ActiveBrief, and synthesis settings shall be read from ragstream/config/runtime_config.json where the implementation currently consumes them.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION
      8.1. Derivative Failure Isolation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Vector-ingestion, retrieval, compression, or synthesis failure shall not rewrite or corrupt existing durable .ragmem content.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION
      8.2. No Fabricated Memory UID: GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-EMPTY AC: Empty Memory Context
      STATEMENT: Memory components shall not invent MemoryRecords, source text, candidate bodies, tags, keys, scores, or synthesized facts when inputs are missing or processing fails.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK
      8.3. Explicit Empty Retrieval Pack UID: GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: When no active history or no query is available, Memory Retrieval shall write an explicit empty MemoryContextPack with a diagnostic reason rather than fabricate context.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE
      8.4. Synthesis Failure Integrity UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: A Memory Context synthesis failure shall produce empty memory_context and failure diagnostics rather than an invented fallback context.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY
      8.5. Credential Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Provider credentials shall remain outside .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite memory metadata, vector metadata, and agent JSON files.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY
      8.6. Public Logging Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Public logs shall report concise statuses and identifiers and shall not expose complete prompts, memory bodies, ActiveBrief text, vector values, or model payloads by default.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY
      8.7. Developer Logging Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed memory payloads and diagnostics shall remain in explicitly enabled confidential developer logging paths.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY
      8.8. Deterministic Component Testability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Filename sanitation, metadata normalization, SQLite lookups, chunk identity, score conversion, recency decay, candidate merging, and empty-state behavior shall be independently testable without an LLM call.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY
      8.9. LLM Boundary Testability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: ActiveBrief and Memory Context LLM calls shall be replaceable with controlled AgentFactory and LLMClient test doubles.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE
      8.10. Memory Provenance UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PROVENANCE AC: Candidate Provenance
      STATEMENT: Every vector hit, episodic candidate, semantic chunk, Direct Recall result, and ActiveBrief contributor shall remain traceable to record_id and, where applicable, file_id.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE
      9.1. Recording and File Ownership UID: GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed MemoryRecord, .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, capture, file lifecycle, and import requirements shall be owned by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE
      9.2. Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership UID: GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed vector-entry, memory-store, ingestion, deterministic lookup, semantic scoring, candidate selection, and MemoryContextPack requirements shall be owned by 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE
      9.3. ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership UID: GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed sentence reduction, ActiveBrief relevance routing, pending-topic handling, runtime compression, and Memory Context synthesis requirements shall be owned by 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE
      9.4. PreProcessing Ownership Reference UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Prompt parsing, ActiveBrief relation classification, and memory-context permission policy shall remain owned by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE
      9.5. Knowledge Management Ownership Reference UID: GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Document ingestion, document retrieval/reranking, document A3, and document A4 shall remain owned by files 40–43.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE
      9.6. Prompt Composition Ownership Reference UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Final LLM-ready prompt assembly shall remain outside files 50–53.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT
      9.7. Blackboard Current Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-NO-FUTURE-LEAK AC: No Future Feature Leakage
      STATEMENT: No current Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard state model, or Blackboard background-agent workflow shall be claimed as implemented by files 50–53.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT
      9.8. Knowledge Graph Current Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Knowledge-graph memory representation or graph-based retrieval shall not be claimed as current Memory Management behavior.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT
      9.9. Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Automatic overlap detection and removal between final document context and final memory context shall remain outside the current implementation baseline.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT
      9.10. Background Agent Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Background agents for requirement-code synchronization, autonomous memory maintenance, or unattended context repair shall not be claimed as current Memory Management behavior.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY
      9.11. Code Traceability Direction UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: Memory implementation units shall be traceable to the deepest applicable UID in files 51–53 rather than only to the top-level Memory Management root.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC
      9.12. Requirement-Code Synchronization UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-SYNC AC: Requirement-Code Consistency
      STATEMENT: An approved change to memory truth, persistence, indexing, retrieval policy, score semantics, runtime artifacts, or observable output shall update requirements, architecture, implementation traceability, and verification together.

      Pure internal refactoring that preserves approved behavior shall not require artificial requirement changes.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY
      10.1. AC: Memory Hierarchy UID: GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      STATEMENT: The approved requirements set shall show durable recording, derivative ingestion/retrieval, ActiveBrief/compression/synthesis, and final prompt handoff as separate owned responsibilities.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Management Scope UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary

      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position

      • GHOST-MEMORY-EPISODIC-ROLE Episodic Candidate Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SEMANTIC-ROLE Semantic Chunk Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DIRECT-ROLE Direct Recall Candidate Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE ActiveRetrievalBrief Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIZED-ROLE Synthesized Memory Context Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-CORE Core Recording Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-STORAGE File Storage Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-IMPORT Importer Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-INGESTION Ingestion Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-RETRIEVAL Retrieval Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-COMPRESSION Compression and ActiveBrief Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS Synthesis Location
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS ActiveBrief Agent Configuration Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI Runtime and GUI Wiring Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA Runtime Data Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT Runtime Configuration Source
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION Derivative Failure Isolation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated Memory
      • GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK Explicit Empty Retrieval Pack
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE Synthesis Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY Credential Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY Public Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY Developer Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY Deterministic Component Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY LLM Boundary Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE Memory Provenance
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE Recording and File Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE PreProcessing Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE Knowledge Management Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE Prompt Composition Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT Blackboard Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT Knowledge Graph Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT Background Agent Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

    The current Python package namespace may remain ragstream in implementation paths.

    Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS
    1.3. Memory Management Child Documents UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: The Memory Management requirement family shall be decomposed into:
    1. 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
    2. 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
    3. 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc.

    Each child shall own atomic requirements for its assigned capability and shall not duplicate sibling ownership.

    GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE
    1.4. Requirement Nature UID: GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: This document shall define approved behavior, interfaces, boundaries, artifacts, configuration authority, and verification expectations.

    It shall not be used as an implementation-status log or as permission to infer unimplemented behavior.

      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS Synthesis Location
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS ActiveBrief Agent Configuration Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI Runtime and GUI Wiring Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA Runtime Data Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT Runtime Configuration Source
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION Derivative Failure Isolation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated Memory
      • GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK Explicit Empty Retrieval Pack
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE Synthesis Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY Credential Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY Public Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY Developer Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY Deterministic Component Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY LLM Boundary Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE Memory Provenance
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE Recording and File Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE PreProcessing Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE Knowledge Management Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE Prompt Composition Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT Blackboard Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT Knowledge Graph Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT Background Agent Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES
    1.5. Current Executable Memory Capabilities UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: The current executable Memory Management scope shall include manual pair capture, memory-history files, editable metadata, SQLite indexing, ChatGPT shared-link import, memory-vector ingestion, working/episodic/semantic/Direct Recall selection, ActiveRetrievalBrief maintenance, runtime episodic compression, memory synthesis, and SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION
    1.6. No Final Answer Generation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: Memory Management shall produce supporting runtime context and shall not answer the user request or own final LLM answer generation.

    2. 2. Architectural Position and Peer Boundaries
    The approved high-level flow is PreProcessing followed by logically independent Knowledge Management and Memory Management context paths, followed by final prompt composition.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Management Scope UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS Memory Management Child Documents
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE Requirement Nature
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES Current Executable Memory Capabilities

      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION
    2.1. Memory Architectural Position UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-DEPENDENCY PreProcessing Dependency
    • GHOST-MEMORY-FINAL-COMPOSITION Final Prompt Composition Boundary
    STATEMENT: Memory Management shall consume the prepared SuperPrompt after PreProcessing and shall produce a memory-specific runtime context for final prompt composition.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER
      2.2. Knowledge Management Peer Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION Current Serial Execution
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE Document Candidate Independence
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PEER AC: Knowledge Peer Independence
      STATEMENT: Memory Management and Knowledge Management shall be architectural peers.

      Neither path shall require the selected output of the other path in order to execute its current selection logic.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-DEPENDENCY
      2.4. PreProcessing Dependency UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-DEPENDENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
      STATEMENT: Memory retrieval shall consume the effective retrieval query and ActiveBrief routing permissions prepared by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-FINAL-COMPOSITION
      2.8. Final Prompt Composition Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-FINAL-COMPOSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
      STATEMENT: Final prompt composition shall render synthesized memory_context_text as supporting background context.

      Memory Management shall not own the final ordering of every SuperPrompt section or provider submission.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION
      2.1. Memory Architectural Position UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-DEPENDENCY PreProcessing Dependency
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FINAL-COMPOSITION Final Prompt Composition Boundary
      STATEMENT: Memory Management shall consume the prepared SuperPrompt after PreProcessing and shall produce a memory-specific runtime context for final prompt composition.
    GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER
    2.2. Knowledge Management Peer Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION Current Serial Execution
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE Document Candidate Independence
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PEER AC: Knowledge Peer Independence
    STATEMENT: Memory Management and Knowledge Management shall be architectural peers.

    Neither path shall require the selected output of the other path in order to execute its current selection logic.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION
      2.3. Current Serial Execution UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
      STATEMENT: The current controller may execute document retrieval before memory retrieval within one retrieval action.

      This serial call order shall not redefine Memory Management as a child of document retrieval.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE
      2.5. Document Candidate Independence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-DOCUMENT-MUTATION No Document Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-A3-A4-BOUNDARY Document A3 and A4 Boundary
      STATEMENT: Memory scoring, deterministic lookups, compression, and synthesis shall not depend on document candidate scores, document A3 labels, document A4 classes, or S_CTX_MD.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PEER
      10.2. AC: Knowledge Peer Independence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PEER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
      STATEMENT: Given a prepared SuperPrompt and active memory history, Memory Management shall execute without requiring document retrieval or A3/A4 results.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER
      2.2. Knowledge Management Peer Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION Current Serial Execution
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE Document Candidate Independence
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PEER AC: Knowledge Peer Independence
      STATEMENT: Memory Management and Knowledge Management shall be architectural peers.

      Neither path shall require the selected output of the other path in order to execute its current selection logic.
    GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION
    2.3. Current Serial Execution UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SERIAL-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
    STATEMENT: The current controller may execute document retrieval before memory retrieval within one retrieval action.

    This serial call order shall not redefine Memory Management as a child of document retrieval.

    GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE
    2.5. Document Candidate Independence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-PEER Knowledge Management Peer Boundary
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-DOCUMENT-MUTATION No Document Artifact Mutation
    • GHOST-MEMORY-A3-A4-BOUNDARY Document A3 and A4 Boundary
    STATEMENT: Memory scoring, deterministic lookups, compression, and synthesis shall not depend on document candidate scores, document A3 labels, document A4 classes, or S_CTX_MD.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-DOCUMENT-MUTATION
      2.6. No Document Artifact Mutation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-NO-DOCUMENT-MUTATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE Document Candidate Independence
      STATEMENT: Memory Management shall not mutate document base_context_chunks, document stage views, document final_selection_ids, or S_CTX_MD.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-A3-A4-BOUNDARY
      2.7. Document A3 and A4 Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-A3-A4-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE Document Candidate Independence
      STATEMENT: Current A3 and A4 shall remain document-evidence stages.

      Memory semantic chunks shall not be described as passing through document A3 or A4 in the current architecture.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DOCUMENT-INDEPENDENCE

      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY Developer Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY Deterministic Component Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY LLM Boundary Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE Memory Provenance
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE Recording and File Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE PreProcessing Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE Knowledge Management Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE Prompt Composition Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT Blackboard Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT Knowledge Graph Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT Background Agent Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-STACK-BOUNDARY
    2.9. Agent Stack Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-STACK-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief summarization and Memory Context synthesis shall use the neutral Agent Stack defined in 20_agent_stack.sdoc.

    This Memory requirement family shall own the concrete memory payloads, decisions, safeguards, and state write-back.

    GHOST-MEMORY-GUI-BOUNDARY
    2.10. GUI Boundary UID: GHOST-MEMORY-GUI-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: The GUI may trigger, display, or edit memory operations, but durable-memory semantics and retrieval policy shall remain owned by server-side memory components.

    3. 3. Functional Decomposition and Lifecycle
    Memory Management combines durable recording, derivative ingestion, query-time retrieval, continuity maintenance, and runtime synthesis without replacing durable memory truth.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT

    GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY
    3.1. Memory Recording Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE Current Capture Sequence
    STATEMENT: Memory Recording shall create and maintain MemoryRecord objects, active history identity, append-only stable-body files, editable metadata files, and a SQLite metadata mirror.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE
      3.9. Current Capture Sequence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY Memory Recording Capability
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE Durable Save Before Vector Ingestion
      STATEMENT: The current pair-capture sequence shall synchronize editable GUI metadata, create a new MemoryRecord, build its ActiveRetrievalBrief, append the stable body to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, refresh SQLite, and then optionally schedule vector ingestion.

    STATEMENT: Memory file operations shall list, create, load, rename, and delete memory histories using stable file_id identity and mutable filenames.

    GHOST-MEMORY-IMPORT-CAPABILITY
    3.3. Conversation Import Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-IMPORT-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: The current import capability shall convert one public ChatGPT shared-link conversation into ordinary MemoryRecord objects and persist it as an ordinary memory history.

      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-INGESTION-CAPABILITY
    3.4. Memory Ingestion Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-INGESTION-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: Memory Ingestion shall derive record-handle, question, and answer vector entries from durable MemoryRecord content and store them in the dedicated memory vector store.

    GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY
    3.5. Memory Retrieval Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE Current Retrieval Sequence
    STATEMENT: Memory Retrieval shall combine semantic-vector results with deterministic SQLite/.ragmem lookups into a structured MemoryContextPack.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE
      3.10. Current Retrieval Sequence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY Memory Retrieval Capability
      STATEMENT: The current memory retrieval sequence shall build a query, run semantic retrieval and deterministic lookups, score and merge candidates, compress episodic candidates, synthesize Memory Context, build diagnostics, and write memory artifacts into SuperPrompt.

    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief maintenance shall create or update a compact query-independent conversation brief when a MemoryRecord is captured and shall preserve contributor traceability.

    GHOST-MEMORY-COMPRESSION-CAPABILITY
    3.7. Runtime Compression Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-COMPRESSION-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: Runtime compression shall reduce selected episodic Q/A evidence against the effective query without modifying durable MemoryRecord text.

    GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-CAPABILITY
    3.8. Memory Context Synthesis Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: Memory synthesis shall produce one compact query-relevant memory_context string from the current configured evidence inputs.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY
      3.1. Memory Recording Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE Current Capture Sequence
      STATEMENT: Memory Recording shall create and maintain MemoryRecord objects, active history identity, append-only stable-body files, editable metadata files, and a SQLite metadata mirror.
    GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE
    3.9. Current Capture Sequence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY Memory Recording Capability
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE Durable Save Before Vector Ingestion
    STATEMENT: The current pair-capture sequence shall synchronize editable GUI metadata, create a new MemoryRecord, build its ActiveRetrievalBrief, append the stable body to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, refresh SQLite, and then optionally schedule vector ingestion.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE
      3.11. Durable Save Before Vector Ingestion UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE Current Capture Sequence
      STATEMENT: A MemoryRecord shall be durably saved before its vector ingestion is scheduled.

      Failure to schedule or complete vector ingestion shall not invalidate the already saved MemoryRecord truth.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY
      3.5. Memory Retrieval Capability UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE Current Retrieval Sequence
      STATEMENT: Memory Retrieval shall combine semantic-vector results with deterministic SQLite/.ragmem lookups into a structured MemoryContextPack.
    GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE
    3.10. Current Retrieval Sequence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-SEQUENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY Memory Retrieval Capability
    STATEMENT: The current memory retrieval sequence shall build a query, run semantic retrieval and deterministic lookups, score and merge candidates, compress episodic candidates, synthesize Memory Context, build diagnostics, and write memory artifacts into SuperPrompt.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE
      3.9. Current Capture Sequence UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY Memory Recording Capability
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE Durable Save Before Vector Ingestion
      STATEMENT: The current pair-capture sequence shall synchronize editable GUI metadata, create a new MemoryRecord, build its ActiveRetrievalBrief, append the stable body to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, refresh SQLite, and then optionally schedule vector ingestion.
    GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE
    3.11. Durable Save Before Vector Ingestion UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DURABLE-BEFORE-DERIVATIVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CAPTURE-SEQUENCE Current Capture Sequence
    STATEMENT: A MemoryRecord shall be durably saved before its vector ingestion is scheduled.

    Failure to schedule or complete vector ingestion shall not invalidate the already saved MemoryRecord truth.

    4. 4. Authority and Data Truth Model
    The memory subsystem deliberately separates authoritative stable content, current editable metadata, lookup indexes, vector derivatives, and run-local context.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT

      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-IMPORT Importer Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-INGESTION Ingestion Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-RETRIEVAL Retrieval Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-COMPRESSION Compression and ActiveBrief Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS Synthesis Location
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS ActiveBrief Agent Configuration Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI Runtime and GUI Wiring Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA Runtime Data Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT Runtime Configuration Source
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION Derivative Failure Isolation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated Memory
      • GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK Explicit Empty Retrieval Pack
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE Synthesis Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY Credential Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY Public Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY Developer Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY Deterministic Component Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY LLM Boundary Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE Memory Provenance
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE Recording and File Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE PreProcessing Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE Knowledge Management Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE Prompt Composition Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT Blackboard Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT Knowledge Graph Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT Background Agent Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL
    4.1. Memory Truth Model UID: GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-STABLE-BODY-AUTHORITY Stable Body Authority
    • GHOST-MEMORY-METADATA-AUTHORITY Editable Metadata Authority
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SQLITE-MIRROR SQLite Mirror Status
    • GHOST-MEMORY-VECTOR-DERIVATIVE Vector Store Derivative Status
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PACK-RUNTIME-ONLY MemoryContextPack Runtime Status
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CONTEXT-RUNTIME-ONLY Synthesized Memory Context Status
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PENDING-BUFFER-RUNTIME Pending Topic Buffer Status
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ONE-ACTIVE-HISTORY Single Active Memory History
    • GHOST-MEMORY-IDENTITY-SEPARATION File Identity and Filename Separation
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PARENT-TRACE Record Parent Trace
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-TRUTH AC: Durable Truth Separation
    STATEMENT: Full accepted input/output MemoryRecord content in .ragmem shall remain the durable memory-body authority.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-STABLE-BODY-AUTHORITY
      4.2. Stable Body Authority UID: GHOST-MEMORY-STABLE-BODY-AUTHORITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: Stable identity, timestamps, input/output text, source, hashes, and stored ActiveRetrievalBrief fields shall be loaded from .ragmem.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-METADATA-AUTHORITY
      4.3. Editable Metadata Authority UID: GHOST-MEMORY-METADATA-AUTHORITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-METADATA AC: Metadata Overlay
      STATEMENT: Current editable tags, retrieval source mode, Direct Recall Key, user keywords, automatic keywords, project name, and embedded-file snapshot shall be represented in .ragmeta.json and overlaid onto loaded MemoryRecords.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-SQLITE-MIRROR
      4.4. SQLite Mirror Status UID: GHOST-MEMORY-SQLITE-MIRROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: memory_index.sqlite3 shall mirror file-level and record-level metadata for deterministic lookup and shall not replace .ragmem as full Q/A truth.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-VECTOR-DERIVATIVE
      4.5. Vector Store Derivative Status UID: GHOST-MEMORY-VECTOR-DERIVATIVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: Memory vectors, embedded documents, vector metadata, and similarity results shall be derivative retrieval aids and shall not replace MemoryRecord truth.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PACK-RUNTIME-ONLY
      4.6. MemoryContextPack Runtime Status UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PACK-RUNTIME-ONLY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-PACK AC: Candidate Pack Separation
      STATEMENT: MemoryContextPack shall be run-local retrieval output and shall not be persisted as durable memory truth.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-CONTEXT-RUNTIME-ONLY
      4.7. Synthesized Memory Context Status UID: GHOST-MEMORY-CONTEXT-RUNTIME-ONLY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: memory_context_text shall be query-dependent runtime context and shall not be written back into .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or the memory vector store.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PENDING-BUFFER-RUNTIME
      4.8. Pending Topic Buffer Status UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PENDING-BUFFER-RUNTIME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: pending_activebrief_topic_buffer shall remain RAM-only and shall not be persisted in .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or vectors.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ONE-ACTIVE-HISTORY
      4.9. Single Active Memory History UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ONE-ACTIVE-HISTORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: One MemoryManager instance shall own one active memory history and its in-memory record list at a time.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-IDENTITY-SEPARATION
      4.10. File Identity and Filename Separation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-IDENTITY-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: file_id shall be the stable memory-history identity.

      filename_ragmem and filename_meta shall be mutable path/display attributes and shall not become the identity key.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-PARENT-TRACE
      4.11. Record Parent Trace UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PARENT-TRACE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: A MemoryRecord may carry parent_id to preserve a chain relationship without changing record_id identity.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-TRUTH
      10.3. AC: Durable Truth Separation UID: GHOST-MEMORY-AC-TRUTH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      STATEMENT: A vector-store deletion or empty retrieval run shall not delete or rewrite the corresponding .ragmem MemoryRecord body.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL
      4.1. Memory Truth Model UID: GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

    4.11. Record Parent Trace UID: GHOST-MEMORY-PARENT-TRACE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

    5. 5. Runtime Candidate and Context Model
    Memory retrieval preserves several candidate roles because recent continuity, semantic similarity, explicit priority, and exact recall are not equivalent selection mechanisms.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Management Scope UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHILD-DOCUMENTS Memory Management Child Documents
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REQUIREMENT-NATURE Requirement Nature
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CURRENT-CAPABILITIES Current Executable Memory Capabilities
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION No Final Answer Generation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURAL-POSITION Memory Architectural Position
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AGENT-STACK-BOUNDARY Agent Stack Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-GUI-BOUNDARY GUI Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RECORDING-CAPABILITY Memory Recording Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FILE-CAPABILITY Memory File Lifecycle Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-IMPORT-CAPABILITY Conversation Import Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGESTION-CAPABILITY Memory Ingestion Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-RETRIEVAL-CAPABILITY Memory Retrieval Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPABILITY ActiveRetrievalBrief Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-COMPRESSION-CAPABILITY Runtime Compression Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-CAPABILITY Memory Context Synthesis Capability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-TRUTH-MODEL Memory Truth Model
      • GHOST-MEMORY-WORKING-ROLE Working Memory Candidate Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-EPISODIC-ROLE Episodic Candidate Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SEMANTIC-ROLE Semantic Chunk Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DIRECT-ROLE Direct Recall Candidate Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE ActiveRetrievalBrief Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIZED-ROLE Synthesized Memory Context Role
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-CORE Core Recording Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-STORAGE File Storage Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-IMPORT Importer Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-INGESTION Ingestion Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-RETRIEVAL Retrieval Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-COMPRESSION Compression and ActiveBrief Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-SYNTHESIS Synthesis Location
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-AGENTS ActiveBrief Agent Configuration Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-UI Runtime and GUI Wiring Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LOCATION-DATA Runtime Data Locations
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CONFIG-ROOT Runtime Configuration Source
      • GHOST-MEMORY-FAILURE-ISOLATION Derivative Failure Isolation
      • GHOST-MEMORY-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated Memory
      • GHOST-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK Explicit Empty Retrieval Pack
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS-FAILURE Synthesis Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-MEMORY-SECRET-BOUNDARY Credential Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PUBLIC-LOG-BOUNDARY Public Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DEVELOPER-LOG-BOUNDARY Developer Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-DETERMINISTIC-TESTABILITY Deterministic Component Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-LLM-TESTABILITY LLM Boundary Testability
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROVENANCE Memory Provenance
      • GHOST-MEMORY-REC-FILE-REFERENCE Recording and File Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-INGEST-RETRIEVE-REFERENCE Ingestion and Retrieval Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-REFERENCE ActiveBrief and Merge Ownership
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PREPROCESS-REFERENCE PreProcessing Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KM-REFERENCE Knowledge Management Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-PROMPT-COMPOSITION-REFERENCE Prompt Composition Ownership Reference
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BLACKBOARD-NOT-CURRENT Blackboard Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH-NOT-CURRENT Knowledge Graph Current Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CROSS-OVERLAP-NOT-CURRENT Cross-Context Deduplication Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-BACKGROUND-AGENTS-NOT-CURRENT Background Agent Boundary
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CODE-TRACEABILITY Code Traceability Direction
      • GHOST-MEMORY-CHANGE-SYNC Requirement-Code Synchronization
      • GHOST-MEMORY-AC-HIERARCHY AC: Memory Hierarchy
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide a Memory Management capability that records accepted input/output pairs, persists memory histories, indexes memory metadata, creates dedicated memory vectors, retrieves multiple memory candidate classes, maintains ActiveRetrievalBrief state, compresses selected evidence, and produces query-dependent Memory Context.

      Memory Management shall remain separate from document Knowledge Management while participating as its logical peer after PreProcessing.
    GHOST-MEMORY-WORKING-ROLE
    5.1. Working Memory Candidate Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-WORKING-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    STATEMENT: Working memory shall represent the latest configured non-excluded records from the active memory history through deterministic SQLite lookup.

    5.4. Direct Recall Candidate Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-DIRECT-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

    GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE
    5.5. ActiveRetrievalBrief Role UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP ActiveBrief Anti-Duplication Reference
    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief shall provide query-independent working-conversation orientation and shall remain distinct from query-dependent Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP
      5.9. ActiveBrief Anti-Duplication Reference UID: GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF-ROLE ActiveRetrievalBrief Role
      STATEMENT: Memory synthesis shall use ActiveRetrievalBrief as orientation and anti-duplication reference rather than as substitute memory evidence.
